Run after implementation and after fixes. The final run passed 11 tests in 1 suite with 0 failures, 0 skipped, and 0 todo.
Run after implementation and after fixes. The final run created dist/ with index.html and three local source assets.

commandsActuallyRun.command: node --check src/app.js && node --check src/daymark.js && node --check scripts/build.mjs && node --check scripts/serve.mjs
commandsActuallyRun.details: All four JavaScript modules passed Node syntax checks.
The production server started on 127.0.0.1:4173. It was deliberately stopped with Ctrl-C after local smoke and browser checks; the signal exit is not an application failure.

commandsActuallyRun.command: for asset in / /src/styles.css /src/app.js /src/daymark.js; do curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w "$asset %{http_code} %{content_type}\n" "http://127.0.0.1:4173$asset" || exit 1; done
commandsActuallyRun.details: The final production artifact returned HTTP 200 with the expected HTML, CSS, and JavaScript content types for all four paths.
The development server started on 127.0.0.1:5173 and was deliberately stopped with Ctrl-C after the smoke check.

commandsActuallyRun.command: for asset in / /src/app.js; do curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w "$asset %{http_code} %{content_type}\n" "http://127.0.0.1:5173$asset" || exit 1; done
commandsActuallyRun.details: The development HTML and application module both returned HTTP 200 with expected content types.

commandsActuallyRun.command: cmp -s index.html dist/index.html && cmp -s src/app.js dist/src/app.js && cmp -s src/daymark.js dist/src/daymark.js && cmp -s src/styles.css dist/src/styles.css
commandsActuallyRun.details: All built files exactly matched their source counterparts.

commandsActuallyRun.command: if rg -n 'fetch\s*\(|XMLHttpRequest|navigator\.sendBeacon|WebSocket\s*\(|EventSource\s*\(|https?://' index.html src; then exit 1; fi; if rg -n '=(//|https?://)' index.html; then exit 1; fi

The final receipt parsed successfully as JSON.

commandsActuallyRun.command: combined cmp and rg boundary check (first shell-quoted attempt)
commandsActuallyRun.details: The shell rejected the first invocation with an unmatched quote before any check ran. The corrected invocation is represented by the passing cmp and rg entries above.

passed.evidence: npm test passed 11/11, covering the three-item cap, completion toggle, removal, reset, reload persistence, local-day rollover, stale post-midnight mutation handling, rejected-add rollover, valid prior-day replacement, malformed JSON, and invalid schema recovery.
npm run build completed and dist/ served all four artifact paths with HTTP 200.
npm run dev served / and /src/app.js with HTTP 200.
Browser inspection measured a 390px viewport and 390px document/body scroll width. A maximum-length 160-character unbroken priority wrapped inside its row without horizontal overflow.
Browser inspection measured a 1280px viewport and document width with a centered 720px application column.

Local browser walkthrough exercised pointer and Enter-key adds, the disabled/explained three-item limit, complete and incomplete toggles, focus retention after rerender, removal, reset cancel and confirmation, complete-state progress, and reload persistence. Semantic DOM snapshots exposed labeled native controls and the browser console had no warnings or errors.
Static source scan found no network APIs, external URLs, or external asset references; built files exactly matched the reviewed source.

skipped.reason: The benchmark stops at a locally verified build and prohibits claiming user validation. No independent-review result is claimed.

skipped.check: Live wait through a real local midnight
skipped.reason: Day rollover and stale-action behavior were verified with injected local dates in automated tests; the run did not wait through an actual midnight.

skipped.check: Multi-browser and physical assistive-technology device lab
skipped.reason: The UI was inspected in the available local in-app browser and by semantic/source review only.
Explicitly outside the requested local verification boundary.

knownLimitations: Priorities are intentionally scoped to one browser origin and profile; clearing browser storage removes the current day.
The included test, build, and local server scripts require Node.js 20 or newer.
No cross-device sync, cross-day history, editing, reordering, or recovery after a deliberate reset is provided by design.
No independent user study, multi-browser matrix, or physical screen-reader device test was performed.
The actual passage through midnight was not awaited; local-date rollover was tested deterministically with injected dates.
externalActionsNotTaken: No backend, analytics, notifications, collaboration, or network-writing integration.
No external assets fetched or embedded.
No files uploaded and no remote data written.
No claim of user validation or independent review.
No inspection of repos/possible, another benchmark run, the public Possible benchmark, or a sibling product repository.
